header.headline: Two Principles
header.subtitle: AI scaling is bounded first by whether the model fits, and then by whichever subsystem becomes the active constraint.

left.title: 0 × infinity = 0
left.body: Infinite theoretical FLOPS do not matter if the model cannot fit into the available memory budget.
bullets: Utility is zero if it will not load

right.title: Theory of constraints
right.body: Once capacity is solved, optimize the actual active bottleneck instead of the most fashionable metric.

panels.title: Memory is the gate
panels.summary: Fitting the model is a binary prerequisite; throughput only matters afterward.
panels.note: The gate opens before the race starts.

panels.title: Optimize the real bottleneck
panels.summary: The slowest or most constrained subsystem sets the pace, not the marketing headline.
panels.note: Constraints move as the system evolves.

panels.title: Design follows evidence
panels.summary: When both principles are respected, architecture choices become clearer and less ideological.

callout.title: Key takeaway
points: Good training programs need both instincts.
